definition: The arising or originating condition of name-and-form within dependent-arising analysis.
notes: Use this record when the origin-side compound is named compactly as namarupa-samudaya rather than quoted as a fuller origin-line sentence. Keep the compound aligned with the headword defaults for namarupa and samudaya, and do not flatten it into body-and-mind language or generic arising-language by habit.

example_phrases.notes: Compact origin-side compound in the dependent-arising family.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use for the compact named namarupa-samudaya compound inside the origin-side dependent-arising family; prefer fuller origin-line wording when the canonical sentence itself is present.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not paraphrase the compound into body-and-mind language or generic arising-language when the compact doctrinal form already fits the passage.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents name-and-form origin language from drifting into loose mind-and-body paraphrase or ungoverned arising-language.
